description_long: This is a lifelike representation of an orca, also known as a killer whale, with a robust, streamlined body and a striking black-and-white coloration. The dorsal surface is predominantly black, while the ventral side and lower jaw are white, with a distinctive white eye patch above and behind each eye. The dorsal fin is tall and triangular, and the pectoral fins are broad and paddle-shaped. The tail flukes are wide and slightly curved, and the animal's head is rounded with a pronounced beakless snout. The overall form is muscular and hydrodynamic, suited for powerful swimming. The coloration and markings are highly characteristic of the species, aiding in identification.
description: A robust, black-and-white orca with a tall dorsal fin, broad pectoral fins, and distinctive white markings. Its streamlined body and unique coloration are highly recognizable.
description_view_0: Side view showing the orca's full body profile, tall dorsal fin, and clear black-and-white color pattern with a visible white eye patch.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rounded head, symmetrical pectoral fins, and the dorsal fin rising from the back.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the orca's other flank, with similar markings and the tail flukes visible at the rear.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the broad tail flukes and the tapering body, with the dorsal fin and pectoral fins extending outward.
